boundaries.note: shadow re-ranks the SAME base_score candidates; changes the RCA
boundaries.recommendation: MORE_EVIDENCE — no divergence yet on this corpus

boundaries.note: evidence_confidence vs calibrated confidence; changes a number
boundaries.recommendation: MORE_EVIDENCE — no divergence yet on this corpus

boundaries.note: baseline emits no service localization; T3 adds one
boundaries.recommendation: SHADOW_FIRST — additive capability; measure usefulness in pilot

boundaries.note: gates a weak conclusion; never changes the winner
boundaries.recommendation: SAFE_FIRST_CANDIDATE — read-only gating, lowest risk; A/B once labeled

boundaries.note: identifies decisive evidence; no authoritative counterpart
boundaries.recommendation: SHADOW_FIRST — additive capability; measure usefulness in pilot

limitations: leverage measures where a shadow signal WOULD change/add to the authoritative decision; whether that change is an IMPROVEMENT still requires labeled outcomes
why_identical: structural: the shadow re-scores the baseline's OWN ranked candidate set (no independent hypothesis generation), so it rarely overturns the base_score argmax on the ranking boundary
contractual: every decision boundary is non-authoritative — the shadow signals are wired to nothing, so they cannot influence the outcome
evidential: corpus n=3 is small/homogeneous and may not exercise cases where re-ranking would flip the winner
observed: 0 ranking divergences on this corpus — the baseline's top candidate already matches the shadow's evidence-ranked winner
